evals.prompt: Create a new FastAPI service named 'inventory_api' in /tmp/fastapi_eval_inventory_api. It needs typed environment settings, a /health endpoint, request ID middleware, browser CORS support for local frontend development, pytest coverage, and Docker Compose for local container testing.
evals.expected_output: A greenfield FastAPI service with app/main.py, pydantic-settings config, versioned API router package, /health test coverage, request ID middleware, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and direct Uvicorn/FastAPI startup commands.
expectations: The project contains app/main.py exporting a FastAPI app and registering routers in one place
app/core/config.py defines typed settings with pydantic-settings and a cached settings getter
A GET /health endpoint is implemented and covered by tests/test_health.py
Request ID middleware is present and the health test checks for an x-request-id header
Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml are generated because container testing was requested
The documented default startup path uses direct Uvicorn or FastAPI CLI commands instead of a wrapper script
Generated production/container configuration does not use the deprecated uvicorn.workers module

evals.prompt: Set up a new FastAPI service called 'reports_api' in /tmp/fastapi_eval_reports_api. I don't need Docker, but I do need a clean pattern for long-lived process resources because later we'll add a search client and background indexer. Please include the lifespan/app.state/dependency-accessor pattern and a minimal test suite.
evals.expected_output: A FastAPI project without Docker files, using lifespan as the composition root, app.state for process-level resources/settings, typed dependency accessors, and a health test.
expectations: app/main.py defines or documents a lifespan function for process-level resources
app/api/v1/dependencies.py contains typed dependency accessors rather than endpoint-level service construction
No Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml is added because Docker was not requested
No gunicorn.conf.py is added because a separate process manager was not requested
The generated README includes direct local run and test commands
The scaffold keeps baseline tests independent of external services

evals.prompt: Modernize the existing FastAPI project in /tmp/fastapi_eval_legacy_service. It currently has a single app.py with GET /health returning {"status":"ok"}, GET /items returning [{"id":1,"name":"Widget"},{"id":2,"name":"Gadget"}], plus run_service.py that shells out to uvicorn. Keep those exact endpoint paths and response payloads, move to a standard app/main.py layout with routers, add typed settings, tests, and replace the wrapper launcher with documented direct startup commands.
evals.expected_output: The legacy FastAPI app is refactored into the standard layout without losing the exact /items or /health behavior; run_service.py is removed or deprecated in favor of direct commands; tests cover the preserved endpoints and assert the Widget/Gadget payload.
expectations: Existing /items behavior is preserved in the modernized app
The ASGI app is exposed from app/main.py
Routes are split into router modules instead of remaining in one app.py file
run_service.py is removed or clearly replaced by documented direct Uvicorn commands
Tests cover both /health and the preserved /items endpoint
The modernization preserves the existing dependency manager when one is present
